name: Residential Research Unit Design
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant comfort → Systemic: Enhanced data quality through naturalistic behavior → Strategic: Improved research validity and generalizability.
options: Domestic-style suites with minimal clinical cues
Modular, adaptable design for future expansion and technology integration
Themed environment to mimic real-world settings and induce specific parasomnia triggers
review: Controls participant comfort vs. experimental control. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of environmental stimuli on parasomnia episodes.

name: Sensor Acquisition Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Reduced participant burden → Systemic: Increased data accuracy through tiered monitoring approach → Strategic: Enhanced research efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
options: Scheduled enhanced nights with fuller PSG montage
Event-driven escalation to full PSG after technician-flagged events
Continuous, real-time monitoring with AI-powered anomaly detection
review: Controls participant burden vs. data quality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of AI-powered anomaly detection on parasomnia episode identification.

name: Participant Recruitment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased enrollment rates → Systemic: Improved participant diversity and representation → Strategic: Enhanced research generalizability and validity.
options: Targeted recruitment through sleep clinics and neurologist referrals
Online advertising and social media outreach to reach a broader audience
Collaboration with patient advocacy groups to leverage their networks
review: Controls participant enrollment vs. diversity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of online recruitment on parasomnia episode frequency and severity.

name: Data Sharing and Governance
consequences: Immediate: Increased transparency and collaboration → Systemic: Improved data reuse and reproducibility → Strategic: Enhanced research impact and credibility.
options: Public data sharing through a recognized repository
Limited data sharing with select partners and collaborators
Strict data governance and control, with only internal use
review: Controls data access vs. collaboration. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of public data sharing on participant privacy and consent.

name: Personnel and Budget Allocation
consequences: Immediate: Increased staff efficiency → Systemic: Improved resource allocation and management → Strategic: Enhanced research productivity and sustainability.
options: Core team of experienced researchers and technicians
Graduate student involvement for data analysis and publication support
Pharmaceutical industry partnership for funding and expertise
review: Controls staff efficiency vs. cost-effectiveness. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of pharmaceutical industry partnerships on research independence and integrity.

name: Operational Scalability Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant enrollment → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and annotation throughput → Strategic: Improved research productivity and grant competitiveness.
options: Expand to accommodate up to 12 active suites, allowing for a larger sample size and increased event yield.
Implement a tiered recruitment model, prioritizing participants with higher parasomnia episode frequencies.
Develop a predictive analytics tool to forecast participant enrollment and optimize staffing ratios.
review: Controls Data quality vs. Participant retention. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of participant burnout on data quality.

name: Sensor Integration Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Enhanced PSG capabilities → Systemic: Increased event detection and capture → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential.
options: Integrate a full-body EMG system to capture muscle activity and enhance parasomnia episode detection.
Implement a machine learning-based sensor fusion algorithm to combine data from multiple sources.
Develop a custom-designed sensing platform utilizing advanced signal processing techniques.
review: Controls Sensing capabilities vs. Data storage and retention. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of sensor noise on data quality.

name: Personnel Development Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Improved research technician training → Systemic: Enhanced annotation efficiency and accuracy → Strategic: Increased research productivity and grant competitiveness.
options: Develop a comprehensive training program for research technicians, focusing on parasomnia episode identification and annotation.
Implement a mentorship model, pairing experienced researchers with junior staff members to enhance skills and knowledge transfer.
Create a personnel development plan, prioritizing ongoing education and professional growth for all team members.
review: Controls Personnel training vs. Research output. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of burnout on research technician morale and productivity.

name: Data Sharing and Governance Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased data sharing → Systemic: Enhanced collaboration and knowledge transfer → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential.
options: Develop a data sharing agreement with the Deutsche Gesellschaft für Schlafforschung und Schlafmedizin, enabling collaboration on parasomnia research.
Create a data governance plan, outlining procedures for data access, use, and protection.
Establish a data repository, providing a centralized location for storing and sharing parasomnia-related data.
review: Controls Data sharing vs. Participant privacy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of data sharing on participant confidentiality.

name: Pharmaceutical Partnership Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased funding and resources → Systemic: Enhanced research capabilities and collaboration → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential.
options: Develop a partnership with a pharmaceutical company, leveraging their expertise and resources for parasomnia research.
Establish a collaborative research agreement, enabling joint research projects and publications.
Create a co-branded research initiative, combining the strengths of both parties to drive innovation and progress.
review: Controls Pharmaceutical partnership vs. Research independence. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of industry influence on research outcomes.

name: Grant Writing and Fundraising Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased funding and resources → Systemic: Enhanced research capabilities and collaboration → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential.
options: Develop a comprehensive grant writing plan, targeting key funding agencies and opportunities.
Establish a fundraising strategy, leveraging the strengths of the research team and network to secure additional resources.
Create a crowdfunding campaign, engaging the public and stakeholders in support of parasomnia research.
review: Controls Grant writing vs. Research output. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of grant writing on research priorities.

name: Research Unit Expansion Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant enrollment and data quality → Systemic: Enhanced research capabilities and collaboration → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential.
options: Expand the research unit to accommodate up to 20 active suites, allowing for a larger sample size and increased event yield.
Develop a modular expansion plan, enabling incremental growth and adaptation to changing research needs.
Create a phased expansion strategy, prioritizing key research areas and objectives.
review: Controls Research unit expansion vs. Staffing ratios. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of expansion on staffing ratios and participant burnout.

name: Participant Screening Protocol
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant recruitment → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and validity → Strategic: Greater confidence in research findings.
options: Structured Clinical Interview
Collateral History from Bed Partner or Household Member
Prior Polysomnography
review: Controls participant selection vs. participant retention.

name: Sensor Data Transmission Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Reduced data transmission latency → Systemic: Improved data integrity and reliability → Strategic: Enhanced research reproducibility.
options: Real-time Data Streaming
Scheduled Batch Processing
Event-driven Data Transfer
review: Weakness: The options fail to consider potential cybersecurity risks.

name: Participant Compensation Model
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant engagement → Systemic: Enhanced participant retention and satisfaction → Strategic: Greater research validity.
options: Flat Rate Per Night
Tiered Compensation Structure
Meal Voucher Program
review: Controls participant compensation vs. participant motivation.

name: Data Sharing and Governance Framework
consequences: Immediate: Increased data sharing and collaboration → Systemic: Enhanced research impact and reputation → Strategic: Greater scientific progress.
options: Open Data Policy
Restricted Access to Sensitive Data
Data Sharing Agreements with Partner Institutions
review: Weakness: The options fail to consider potential privacy concerns.

name: Research Unit Expansion Plan
consequences: Immediate: Increased research capacity and efficiency → Systemic: Enhanced data collection and analysis capabilities → Strategic: Greater research impact.
options: Phased Expansion
Full-scale Renovation and Upgrade
Co-Location with Other Research Units
review: Controls research unit expansion vs. resource allocation.

name: Staff Training and Development Program
consequences: Immediate: Improved staff knowledge and skills → Systemic: Enhanced research quality and productivity → Strategic: Greater research impact.
options: Regular Workshops and Training Sessions
Mentorship Programs for New Staff Members
Collaboration with External Experts
review: Weakness: The options fail to consider potential staff turnover.

name: Funding and Resource Allocation Model
consequences: Immediate: Increased research funding and resources → Systemic: Enhanced research quality and productivity → Strategic: Greater research impact.
options: Competitive Bidding Process
Allocative Budgeting
Priority-based Funding Allocation
review: Controls funding allocation vs. resource utilization.
